skill.name: ux-evaluator
skill.description: Evaluates UI components against UX best practices using a three-part framework for position, visual weight, and spacing.
skill.summary: Review buttons, navigation, forms, and layouts with structured UX evidence.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives after contextual review. The files contain Markdown guidance, fenced examples, inline file references, and one cited source URL, with no executable code, cryptographic API use, network calls, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution occurs inside Markdown fences, output templates, and inline documentation references. No command invocation, script file, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visibly Markdown formatting or documentation text. There is no executable language context or command runner.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Source URL Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a citation to a Balsamiq article in a reference document. It is not fetched, called, or used to transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears under a Source label in a Markdown file. No network-capable code exists in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Documentation
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to ordinary Markdown headings, UX prose, and file references. No MD5, SHA1, encryption library, hashing operation, credential handling, or security-sensitive implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain UX guidance and Markdown syntax only. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code or secret processing.

content.value_statement: Teams often debate UI quality without a shared review method. This skill applies a structured framework to produce clear, evidence-based UX recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates UI components across position, visual weight, and spacing.
Reviews buttons, navigation elements, form controls, and layout patterns.
Compares interface choices against documented UX conventions.
Identifies unclear labels, weak hierarchy, and risky spacing.
limitations: Does not perform automated visual testing or pixel inspection by itself.
Depends on the user providing screenshots, code, or clear UI context.
Does not replace user research, accessibility audits, or analytics.
Reference coverage is focused mainly on common web interface components.

use_cases.title: Review a Header CTA Group
use_cases.description: Evaluate sign-in, sign-up, theme, and utility controls for placement, weight, and spacing.

use_cases.title: Resolve Design Feedback
use_cases.description: Turn subjective comments about a component into a structured verdict and prioritized changes.

use_cases.title: Check UI Before Release
use_cases.description: Audit key interaction elements before shipping a landing page, dashboard, or form.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this button group for UX quality. Evaluate position, visual weight, and spacing, then give a clear verdict.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Against Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this navigation layout against common UX conventions. Focus on action order, utility placement, and hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this form layout and rank the needed UX changes by impact. Include rationale for each priority.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Full Interaction Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this full header and hero interaction area. Identify conflicts between primary actions, utilities, labels, and spacing.

output_examples.input: Review a header with Sign In, Get Started, and a theme toggle placed between them.
output_examples.output: The theme toggle should move away from the primary action group. Sign In should be secondary, the primary signup action should be more prominent, and spacing should separate utilities from conversion actions.

output_examples.input: Check a form footer with Cancel and Submit buttons styled the same.
output_examples.output: The actions need clearer hierarchy. Cancel should be visually secondary, Submit should be the primary action, and destructive or irreversible actions need stronger confirmation.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a mobile navigation where tap targets are 32px high.
output_examples.output: The tap targets are too small for reliable mobile use. Increase touch target height, improve spacing between controls, and preserve clear active-state feedback.

best_practices: Provide screenshots, component code, or exact layout details before asking for a verdict.
Ask for priority levels when several UX issues may compete for attention.
Include the product goal so recommendations match the user journey.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only basis for accessibility compliance decisions.
Do not request aesthetic preferences without connecting them to usability.
Do not ignore user research when it contradicts generic interface conventions.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates UI components using position, visual weight, and spacing as the core review dimensions.

faq.question: Can it review screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes, if the assistant environment can inspect the screenshot or the user describes the visible layout clearly.

faq.question: Does it write frontend code?
faq.answer: It focuses on UX evaluation. It can guide code changes, but implementation depends on the surrounding project.

faq.question: Is it only for buttons?
faq.answer: No. It also supports navigation elements, form controls, spacing, hierarchy, and common interface patterns.

faq.question: Does it replace accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It can flag usability concerns, but dedicated accessibility checks are still required.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
